title: Gooey Australian Rice Flour Pancakes
description: Fresh and indulgent, these slow-cooked rice flour pancakes are a perfect lazy Sunday treat, featuring a gooey center and a crispy exterior. This low-sodium-friendly dish is a delightful twist on traditional Australian pancakes.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour, sugar, and baking powder.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, low-fat milk, and melted unsalted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Add pure vanilla extract and salt-free seasoning blend, and stir gently.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the skillet or griddle.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Loosen the pancakes with a spatula and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Serve the pancakes warm, with a gooey center and a crispy exterior.
Optional: top with fresh fruits, whipped cream, or pure maple syrup.
